comment_text.sn1.74:2.4: The commentary says this refers to a child who testifies on one’s behalf in a court of law. But I think it is about the joy a parent feels when they hear their child speak for the first time. Śatapatha Brāhmaṇa 7.4.2.38 says, “children utter speech” (prajā vācam pravadanti) when one year old.

translation_text.sn1.74:1.1: “What’s the best of things that rise?
translation_text.sn1.74:1.2: And what’s the finest thing to fall?
translation_text.sn1.74:1.3: And what of the things that go forth?
translation_text.sn1.74:2.1: “A seed’s the best of things that rise.
translation_text.sn1.74:2.2: Rain’s the finest thing to fall.
translation_text.sn1.74:2.3: Cattle, of things that go forth.
translation_text.sn1.74:2.4: And a child is the finest speaker.”
translation_text.sn1.74:3.1: “Knowledge is best of things that rise.
translation_text.sn1.74:3.2: Ignorance the finest thing to fall.
translation_text.sn1.74:3.3: The Saṅgha, of things that go forth.
translation_text.sn1.74:3.4: And the Buddha is the finest speaker.”
